zh.Abernant, Carmarthenshire, Wales: 威爾士卡馬特史爾阿波南特
zh.Llansteffan, Carmarthenshire, Wales: 威爾士卡馬特史爾利安斯特凡
zh.lifeSummary: 當Agnes ferch Wilcock Cradock於大約1417年出生在威爾士卡馬特史爾利安斯特凡時，她的父親Sir Wilcock Cradock25歲，母親Elen Ferch Jenkin27歲。她於大約1435年與Llwyd Fychan ap Jenkin結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1441年之後。

zh-hans.Abernant, Carmarthenshire, Wales: 威尔士卡馬特史爾阿波南特
zh-hans.Llansteffan, Carmarthenshire, Wales: 威尔士卡馬特史爾利安斯特凡
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Agnes ferch Wilcock Cradock于大约1417年出生在威尔士卡馬特史爾利安斯特凡时，她的父亲Sir Wilcock Cradock25岁，母亲Elen Ferch Jenkin27岁。她于大约1435年与Llwyd Fychan ap Jenkin结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于1441年之后。

nameMeanings.name: Wilcock
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): from the Middle English personal name Wilcoc, a pet form of William (composed of the elements Will 1 + the hypocoristic suffix -cok; see Cocke ).

variants.name: Wilcockson
meanings: English (Derbyshire and Nottinghamshire): patronymic meaning ‘son of   Wilcock  ’.

variants.name: William
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English personal name William, Willam, Willem, an Old Picard and Norman French form of ancient Germanic Willihelm, composed of the elements wil- ‘will, desire’ + helm- ‘helmet, protection’. This Norman personal name became widely used in England, Scotland and Wales after the Conquest, mainly no doubt in honor of the Conqueror himself. By the 13th century it had already become the most popular personal name in England. A Parisian form of the name Guillaume was also sometimes used (see Gillam ), which in medieval Wales was adopted as Gwilym (see Gwilliam ).
Americanized form (translation into English) of French   Guillaume  .

variants.name: Wilcott
meanings: English (Somerset and Gloucestershire): habitational name from Willicote in Clifford Chambers (Gloucestershire). The placename derives from Old English wilig ‘willow’ + cot ‘cottage, hut, shelter’. In some cases perhaps an altered form of Wilcock .

variants.name: Wilcox
meanings: English (Yorkshire, Lancashire, and Flintshire): variant of Wilcock , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s. This form of the surname is based on the pronunciation.

nameMeanings.name: Cradock
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Craddock
meanings: Welsh and English: from the Welsh personal name Caradawc, Cradawc, Caradoc, Caradog meaning ‘amiable’, familiar in its Latin form Caractacus (but correctly Caratacus, for British Celtic Caratācos, from the stem carat- ‘dear, love’), a British prince who was taken as prisoner to Rome in about 51 AD for his leadership of a revolt against the Roman occupation.
Americanized form of German   Gredig  . Compare   Graddick  .

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

nameMeanings.name: ferch
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: variant of   Ferg  . Compare   Verch  .
Probably also an Americanized form of German Förtsch (see Foertsch ).
Probably also an Americanized form of German Förch (see Foerch ).

variants.name: Ferg
meanings: From the vocabulary word denoting the plant (Old English fearn). Modern use of this word as a given name dates from the 19th century, when it is also recorded occasionally as a boy's name in the United States. In some instances the given name may be a transferred use of the surname derived in the medieval period from the vocabulary word.

en.lifeSummary: When Agnes ferch Wilcock Cradock was born about 1417, in Llansteffan, Carmarthenshire, Wales, her father, Sir Wilcock Cradock, was 25 and her mother, Elen Ferch Jenkin, was 27. She married Llwyd Fychan ap Jenkin about 1435. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 4 daughters. She died after 1441.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Agnes ferch Wilcock Cradock est née environ 1417, à Llansteffan, Carmarthenshire, Galles, son père, Sir Wilcock Cradock, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Elen Ferch Jenkin, avait 27 ans. Elle a épousé Llwyd Fychan ap Jenkin environ 1435. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée après 1441.

de.lifeSummary: Als Agnes ferch Wilcock Cradock um 1417 in Llansteffan, Carmarthenshire, Wales geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Wilcock Cradock 25 und ihre Mutter Elen Ferch Jenkin 27. Sie hat um 1435 Llwyd Fychan ap Jenkin geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie starb nach 1441.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Agnes ferch Wilcock Cradock è nata il circa 1417 nel Llansteffan, Carmarthenshire, Galles, sua padre, Sir Wilcock Cradock, aveva 25 anni e sua madre, Elen Ferch Jenkin, aveva 27 anni. Lei sposato Llwyd Fychan ap Jenkin circa 1435. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 4 figlie. Morì dopo il 1441.

ja.Abernant, Carmarthenshire, Wales: ウェールズカーマースエンシュアイアアバーナント
ja.Llansteffan, Carmarthenshire, Wales: ウェールズカーマースエンシュアイアランステファン
ja.lifeSummary: Agnes ferch Wilcock Cradockが約1417年にウェールズカーマースエンシュアイアランステファンで生まれた時，父Sir Wilcock Cradockは25歳，母Elen Ferch Jenkinは27歳であった。約1435年にLlwyd Fychan ap Jenkinと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女は1441年以後に死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Agnes ferch Wilcock Cradock(이)가 대략 1417년 웨일스 카마테실르 랜스테펀에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Wilcock Cradock(은)는 25세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elen Ferch Jenkin(은)는 27세였다. 그녀는 대략 1435년 Llwyd Fychan ap Jenkin(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1441년 이후 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Agnes ferch Wilcock Cradock nasceu aproximadamente 1417, em Llansteffan, Carmarthenshire, Gales, seu pai, Sir Wilcock Cradock, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Elen Ferch Jenkin, tinha 27 anos. Ela casou-se com Llwyd Fychan ap Jenkin aproximadamente 1435. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 4 filhas. Ela faleceu após 1441.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Agnes ferch Wilcock Cradock родилась около 1417 г. в местности лланстеффань, кармартеншир, Уэльс, её отцу по имени Sir Wilcock Cradock было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Elen Ferch Jenkin, 27. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Llwyd Fychan ap Jenkin около 1435 г. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 4 дочери. Она умерла после 1441 г.

sm.about 1417–after 1441: e tusa o le 1417–ina ua ma'ea le 1441

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Agnes ferch Wilcock Cradock nació aproximadamente 1417, en Llansteffan, Carmarthenshire, Gales, su papá, Sir Wilcock Cradock, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Elen Ferch Jenkin, tenía 27 años. Se casó con Llwyd Fychan ap Jenkin aproximadamente 1435. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió después de 1441.
